taxonomy: Acrocephalus hispidus is a species of bird, specifically a warbler. It belongs to the family Acrocephalidae, which is a group of Old World warblers. This species is further classified into the genus Acrocephalus, which includes various warblers found in Africa, Asia, and Europe. Acrocephalus hispidus is also known as the desert warbler in some regions. Its classification is part of the larger taxonomic hierarchy of birds, which includes class Aves, order Passeriformes, and family Acrocephalidae.
